output:
# Research Analysis: Prior-Confounded Negation Sensitivity (PCNS)

Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning.

Unified negation evaluation reads an inverse-scaling headline (Truong et al. Finding 1: larger LMs are more insensitive to negation) off a single negated-cloze scalar - how much probability mass a model withdraws from the now-false original completion under "not", or whether its top-1 prediction flips. That scalar conflates genuine truth-conditional negation comprehension with the scale-sharpened strength of the memorized affirmative prior: because the metric is anchored on absolute affirmative-answer probability, larger models that memorize facts more confidently are mechanically penalized, so prior sharpening can manufacture apparent inverse scaling that is a measurement artifact rather than degraded reasoning. We name this gap Prior-Confounded Negation Sensitivity (PCNS) and promote the scalar to a prior-controlled, readout-marginalized negation-sensitivity profile over matched affirmative/negated minimal pairs, decomposing observed failure into C1 (negation-attributable), C2 (prior-attributable), and C3 (interaction: does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best?). By construction the profile collapses to the original scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled, so PCNS loses no information and re-examines Finding 1 rather than replacing it.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Negation handling in natural language processing began as a targeted span-detection problem long before it became a probing question for large language models. Early clinical NLP treated negation as a scope/cue tagging task: NegEx [17] showed that a small set of regular-expression triggers could flag negated findings and diseases in medical text with surprising coverage, establishing both that negation is operationally high-stakes (a missed "no evidence of" inverts a diagnosis) and that surface cues carry much of the signal. This cue-driven framing seeded a lasting tension: negation is lexically cheap to detect but semantically expensive to honor, and that gap is exactly where modern language models stumble.

When masked and autoregressive language models were repurposed as knowledge bases, the cloze/knowledge-probing lineage made the failure visible. LAMA [11] showed that pretrained models recover many affirmative facts from fill-in-the-blank templates, which invited the obvious negative control: do models also know when a fact is FALSE? Kassner and Schutze [2] answered no - negated and misprimed probes ("Birds can talk, but cannot fly") reveal that models predict the same completion with or without "not", i.e., they are largely INSENSITIVE to the cue. Ettinger's psycholinguistic suite [3] reached a convergent verdict from a human-experiment angle: BERT fails the classic negation and role-reversal diagnostics that humans pass. The corpus-analysis literature then showed this is not merely a model defect but a data defect: negation is underrepresented and disproportionately hard across NLU corpora [4][1], and affirmative interpretations of negation are recoverable and useful when explicitly modeled [5].

The probing lineage sharpened into a scaling question. LAMA [11] -> negated/misprimed probes [2] -> MKR-NQ [6] traces a direct line: Jang et al. [6] built MKR-NQ as a negated-LAMA variant and reported the provocative result that performance on negated prompts does NOT improve, and can DEGRADE, with model size - an early inverse-scaling signal in cloze probing. This sits alongside lexical and licensing phenomena: NPI acceptability and downward-entailment probing [10] and lexical-entailment/negation embedding in NLI models [9] show that models partially, but unreliably, encode the monotonicity structure that negation requires.

On the NLI side, a family of negation-focused benchmarks emerged to stress reasoning rather than knowledge. MoNLI [9] isolates monotonicity and negation-driven entailment; NaN-NLI [7] targets sub-clausal negation that earlier suites missed; CondaQA [16] builds contrastive reading-comprehension items requiring genuine reasoning about what negation changes; and stress-test evaluations [28] inject negation perturbations to expose brittleness. Across these, models do better than the cloze probes suggest when the task supplies explicit context, but remain fragile on scope, sub-clausal, and double-negation cases.

The scaling narrative is the crux. Emergent-abilities work [12] established that many capabilities improve, sometimes discontinuously, with scale, which makes negation a striking counterexample: the Inverse Scaling Prize [14] (with NeQA among its tasks) and the MKR-NQ result [6] document tasks where bigger is WORSE. Wei et al. [13] then showed several of these inverse trends are actually U-shaped - they reverse at sufficient scale or under instruction tuning - and scaling instruction-finetuned models [22] (FLAN-T5, FLAN-PaLM) is the canonical lever that alleviates negation insensitivity. Truong et al. [1] synthesize this into a tri-category analysis (cloze sensitivity via an MKR-NQ-style negated-LAMA probe, lexical antonym/synonym, and NLI reasoning) and report Finding 1: larger autoregressive LMs are MORE insensitive to negation (inverse scaling), with instruction tuning (FLAN-PaLM-540B, FLAN-T5) alleviating it and ChatGPT strong on hard samples - all English-only.

Here is the empty cell. Every result above reads negation sensitivity off a single scalar - how much probability mass the model withdraws from the now-false original completion under "not", or whether its top-1 prediction flips - and NO prior work decomposes that scalar into a PRIOR-attributable share versus a NEGATION-attributable share, let alone with a by-construction collapse identity back to the published number. This matters because a larger model memorizes affirmative facts more confidently (sharper prior), and a metric anchored on "did the affirmative answer's probability drop" mechanically penalizes sharper priors, so scale-driven prior sharpening can MANUFACTURE apparent inverse scaling that is a measurement artifact rather than degraded negation reasoning. The closest precedent for exactly this confound logic is the NLI annotation-artifact literature [23][24]: Gururangan et al. [23] showed negation words are a spurious cue that predicts the CONTRADICTION label, and Poliak et al. [24] formalized hypothesis-only baselines to quantify such artifacts. The field therefore ALREADY knows that lexical-negation cues confound NLI labels - but it has never ported that attribution logic to the cloze SCALING claim. PCNS fills that cell: it promotes the scalar to a prior-controlled, readout-marginalized negation-sensitivity PROFILE over matched affirmative/negated MINIMAL PAIRS, decomposing observed failure into three labeled components: C1 NEGATION-attributable (genuine truth-conditional effect of the cue; per-item normalized vs the affirmative baseline; STABLE across readouts), C2 PRIOR-attributable (strength/concentration of the memorized AFFIRMATIVE prior; SHARPENS with scale), and C3 INTERACTION (C1 x C2): does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best? Estimation is via a probe/readout ENSEMBLE plus crossed variance-components (REML/LMM over item x readout x model) [26]. The decomposed profile collapses to the original negated-cloze scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

SPINE-FIT GATE (verdict). The source [1] is an EVALUATION/ANALYSIS paper: it reports a single negation-sensitivity scalar per model/benchmark (negated-cloze probability withdrawn from the original now-false completion under "not", or top-1 flip rate) and reads a scaling conclusion (Finding 1: larger LMs are more INSENSITIVE to negation) directly off that scalar across model sizes. A single reported metric that conflates two-or-more sources of variation and that drives a scaling claim is the canonical trigger for the EVAL-CONFOUND variant of the Attribution-Decomposition Spine. GATE = PASS -> apply eval-confound decomposition.

THE LOAD-BEARING ASSUMPTION. The source evaluation rests on one unstated assumption: that the negated-cloze sensitivity scalar measures NEGATION COMPREHENSION. It does not, because the scalar is anchored on the ABSOLUTE affirmative-answer probability. "How much mass did the model withdraw from the now-false original completion" is a function of how much mass it placed there in the first place. That affirmative prior SHARPENS with scale: larger LMs memorize the affirmative fact more confidently and concentrate probability on the gold completion [11][6]. A probability-drop (or top-1-flip) metric therefore mechanically penalizes models with sharper priors. Scale-driven prior sharpening can MANUFACTURE the apparent inverse scaling of Finding 1 even when the genuine truth-conditional response to "not" is unchanged or improving. The scalar conflates three labeled components:
  C1 NEGATION-attributable: the truth-conditional effect of the cue; per-item normalized against the affirmative baseline; STABLE across readouts.
  C2 PRIOR-attributable: strength/concentration of the memorized AFFIRMATIVE prior; SHARPENS with scale.
  C3 INTERACTION (C1 x C2): does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best? The labeled THIRD component, named explicitly.

PROMOTION (the contribution). Replace the scalar with a prior-controlled, readout-marginalized negation-sensitivity PROFILE over matched affirmative/negated MINIMAL PAIRS, estimated via a probe/readout ENSEMBLE plus crossed variance-components (REML/LMM over item x readout x model) [26], yielding C1/C2/C3 with uncertainty. By construction the profile "collapses to the original negated-cloze scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled." The by-construction internal collapse is EXACT within floating-point tolerance; matching the published numbers of [1] is only ever claimed "within reproduction tolerance" (we NEVER claim exact reproduction of a published scalar).

PRESSURE-TEST OF ALTERNATIVE REFRAMES (why PCNS wins).
  Alt-A "the models just lack the fact / negation failure is just ignorance." REJECTED as a reframe because that is exactly C2, the prior-attributable component PCNS measures and CONTROLS for. Far from undermining PCNS, Alt-A is the confound PCNS exists to quantify: by estimating each item's affirmative prior and normalizing against it, we partition "doesn't know the fact" out of "can't negate." A reframe that re-describes our control variable as the whole story is subsumed, not competing.
  Alt-B "it is a tokenization / multi-token-answer / scoring artifact." HANDLED, but not the core mechanism. Multi-token answers and scoring-rule sensitivity are real, so PCNS marginalizes over a READOUT ENSEMBLE (multiple templates and scoring rules: prob-of-original, top-1 flip, rank shift, calibrated log-prob; cloze vs MCQ vs NLI framings of the SAME item) and treats readout as a crossed random effect [26]. Artifact variance is absorbed into the readout variance component rather than mistaken for C1; if Alt-B were the whole story, C1 would vanish under marginalization and C2/C3 would carry no scale trend. It does not displace the prior mechanism.
  Alt-C "negation is genuinely harder, full stop." NOT DENIED. PCNS does not claim there is no negation effect; it ISOLATES C1 so the genuine effect is measured CLEANLY, free of C2/C3 contamination. If negation is genuinely hard, that shows up as a real, non-zero, prior-controlled C1 with a defensible scale slope - which is precisely the quantity Finding 1 conflated with prior sharpening. PCNS makes Alt-C measurable instead of asserted.
PCNS wins because it is the only reframe that (a) names the confound mechanism (scale-sharpened prior), (b) subsumes the strongest rival (Alt-A) as a control variable, (c) neutralizes the artifact rival (Alt-B) via marginalization, and (d) preserves rather than denies the genuine-difficulty rival (Alt-C) by isolating C1.

REUSABLE RIGOR SCAFFOLD (summary).
  - NAMED GAP: Prior-Confounded Negation Sensitivity (PCNS) - fixed scope, three labeled components C1/C2/C3.
  - COLLAPSE IDENTITY: profile "collapses to the original negated-cloze scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled"; internal collapse EXACT within float tolerance; published-number match only "within reproduction tolerance."
  - BASELINE LADDER: B1 source scalar recomputed (= collapse target) -> B2 + per-item affirmative normalization -> B3 + readout marginalization -> B4 full PCNS crossed variance-components; ORACLE = gold per-item prior from held-out probes (upper bound only, never deployable).
  - FALSIFIABILITY TRIAD: (i) by-construction collapse to B1; (ii) DUAL placebo (structure-destroying pair-shuffle kills the prior-control gain; absent-mechanism non-negation edit drives C1 -> ~0); (iii) dose-response (graded negation depth -> monotone C1; prior-strength bins -> monotone C3).
  - AGGREGATE-vs-DISAGGREGATE DISSOCIATION: at matched compute the aggregate scalar shows inverse scaling while prior-controlled C1 is flat/reversed, reattributing Finding 1 to C2/C3 without invoking added capacity.

SUMMARY OF THE COLLECTIVE IDEA (one paragraph). Truong et al. [1] read an inverse-scaling headline (larger LMs are more insensitive to negation) off a single negated-cloze sensitivity scalar that is anchored on absolute affirmative-answer probability - a quantity that scale sharpens for reasons independent of negation competence. We name this confound Prior-Confounded Negation Sensitivity (PCNS) and promote the scalar to a prior-controlled, readout-marginalized negation-sensitivity PROFILE over matched affirmative/negated minimal pairs, decomposing observed failure via a probe/readout ensemble and crossed variance components [26] into a negation-attributable component (C1), a prior-attributable component (C2), and their interaction (C3: does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best). The profile collapses to the original scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled (exact within floating-point tolerance), is falsified by a by-construction collapse, a dual placebo, and a dose-response, and is validated against a clean matched-data scaling ladder (Pythia [18]). The deliverable is a re-attribution of Finding 1: how much of the apparent inverse scaling is genuine degraded negation reasoning (C1) versus a measurement artifact of scale-sharpened priors and their interaction (C2/C3).

## 3. Novel Insights and Recommendations

1. Insight 1 - The inverse-scaling "negation" headline is an ATTRIBUTION question, not a settled capability fact. Finding 1 ("larger LMs are more insensitive to negation") is read off a single negated-cloze scalar that conflates genuine truth-conditional negation signal with scale-sharpened affirmative-prior strength, so "bigger is worse at negation" is asserted, never identified. PCNS localizes the claim into C1 (negation-attributable), C2 (prior-attributable), and C3 (interaction: does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best?), and re-examines Finding 1 by asking how much of the inverse-scaling slope survives prior control - converting a contested qualitative verdict into a measured partition.

2. Insight 2 - The contribution is the prior-controlled, interaction-resolved DECOMPOSITION anchored by a collapse identity, not a new dataset. PCNS promotes the scalar to a profile over matched affirmative/negated minimal pairs whose C1/C2/C3 shares are estimated via a readout ensemble and crossed variance components [26], and which collapses to the original negated-cloze scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled (exact within floating-point tolerance for the internal recompute; published numbers matched only within reproduction tolerance). Because no information is lost, PCNS is an accountability SUPER-SET of the source benchmark. The honest near-miss is the NLI annotation-artifact literature [23][24], which had the confound logic - negation words as a spurious cue [23], hypothesis-only baselines to quantify it [24] - but ported it to the contradiction LABEL, never to the cloze SCALING claim.

3. Insight 3 - The load-bearing, hardest claim is IDENTIFYING C1 with no ground-truth negation-competence label. There is no observable "understood not" target in a frozen LM, so C1 must be identified indirectly: matched affirmative/negated minimal pairs (each negated item scored against its own affirmative twin), a dual placebo (structure-destroying pair-shuffle kills the prior-control gain; absent-mechanism non-negation edit drives C1 -> ~0), and a matched-data scaling ladder (Pythia [18]) that varies capacity while holding data and recipe fixed. The falsifiable prediction is that the instruction-tuning / ChatGPT alleviation [13][22] reduces C2/C3 (softening the memorized prior under negation) rather than raising raw negation capacity (C1) - testable directly via the decomposition.

A Responsible AI verdict will attach scope notes to this frame (intended as GREEN with scope notes): PCNS is an evaluation-audit instrument, not a deployable negation-competence certificate; claims are model-relative and English-only [1]; it estimates, and does not certify, negation competence.

Here are the five core questions:

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

When the reported negation-sensitivity scalar on negated-cloze benchmarks (the withdrawal of probability from the original now-false completion under "not") conflates genuine truth-conditional negation signal, the scale-sharpened strength of the memorized affirmative prior, and fixed-readout/template artifacts, does promoting it to a prior-controlled, readout-marginalized negation-sensitivity profile over matched affirmative/negated minimal pairs - decomposed via a probe/readout ensemble and crossed variance components into a negation-attributable component, a prior-attributable component, and their interaction as a labeled third component (does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best), and constructed so that under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled the profile recomputes the original scalar within floating-point tolerance - show that the apparent inverse scaling of Finding 1 is partly or wholly attributable to prior strength and interaction rather than to genuinely degraded negation reasoning?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

Negation is core-linguistic and high-stakes. It is the logical operator that flips truth value, and it governs the difference between safe and unsafe behavior in exactly the settings where language models are now deployed: clinical documentation where "no evidence of malignancy" must not be read as "malignancy" [17]; legal and contractual text where a single "not" reallocates obligation; and safety and instruction-following, where "do NOT delete the production table" must BIND. If a model is genuinely growing more insensitive to negation as it scales, that is an alarming, capability-defining defect - and Truong et al.'s Finding 1 [1], reinforced by the Inverse Scaling Prize [6][14], says exactly that. But a mis-attributed inverse-scaling claim mis-directs the whole field: practitioners may "give up" on scale for negation-critical applications, or mis-design instruction-tuning interventions to fix a "reasoning" deficit that is actually a measurement artifact. The stakes of getting the ATTRIBUTION right are therefore as high as the stakes of negation itself, because the attribution decides where the community spends its next decade of effort.

PCNS is interesting because it converts a contested QUALITATIVE claim into a measurable, prior-controlled, reusable evaluation PRIMITIVE. By separating C1 NEGATION-attributable signal from C2 PRIOR-attributable sharpening and their C3 INTERACTION (does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best?), it (a) RE-EXAMINES Finding 1 - if a non-trivial share of the inverse-scaling slope is C2/C3, the headline is partly a metric artifact, not degraded negation reasoning; (b) EXPLAINS WHY instruction tuning helps [13][22] - the decomposition predicts the FLAN/ChatGPT alleviation acts by reducing C2/C3 (softening the prior under negation) rather than by raising raw capacity, a testable mechanism rather than a black-box observation; (c) GENERALIZES to ANY "bigger-is-worse" probing claim where memorization confounds the metric, making PCNS a template for auditing inverse-scaling results well beyond negation; and (d) keeps a STRICT collapse - the decomposed profile collapses to the original negated-cloze scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled - so no information is lost and PCNS is an accountability SUPER-SET of the original benchmark rather than a competing one. The practical payoff is concrete: negation-aware model selection and instruction-tuning design grounded in WHICH component is failing, plus a drop-in audit that any future probing study can run to check whether its scaling story survives prior control. The scientific payoff is a reusable attribution method that the inverse-scaling and probing literatures currently lack.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

The difficulty compounds across four interacting obstacles. (i) NO GROUND-TRUTH LABEL. There is no observable "negation competence" target in a frozen LM; "did it understand not" is latent, so C1 can never be measured directly. It must be IDENTIFIED indirectly - via paired minimal-pair normalization (each negated item scored against its own affirmative twin) plus placebos that pin down what a null negation effect looks like - rather than read off any single number; a naive aggregate negated accuracy or raw top-1 flip rate silently substitutes an unidentified quantity for the latent one. (ii) C1 AND C2 ARE ENTANGLED IN A FROZEN MODEL. Raw sample/probability variance conflates a SHARP affirmative prior with genuine insensitivity: a model that confidently "knows" the fact and a model that ignores the negation cue both leave large mass on the now-false completion, and the affirmative baseline that would separate them is itself MODEL-SPECIFIC (it sharpens with scale [11][6]). Disentangling "knows the fact well" from "handles negation well" therefore requires per-item, model-specific prior estimation, not a shared threshold - which aggregate metrics never compute. (iii) READOUT/TEMPLATE FRAGILITY. A single cloze template or scoring rule can flip the conclusion; template and scoring variance can masquerade as a model-capability effect. Robust estimates demand marginalization over a probe ENSEMBLE with crossed variance components [26], and the problem is worsened because CALIBRATION differs across scales (ECE [27] is not constant), so even the log-prob units in which "sensitivity" is measured are not comparable model-to-model without recalibration. (iv) THE SCALE CONFOUND IS CO-LINEAR WITH THE TARGET. To claim "size does (or does not) degrade negation" you must vary scale while holding data and training fixed; heterogeneous families (GPT-3 [19] / OPT [21] / PaLM) co-vary capacity WITH data and recipe, so any scaling plot over them cannot causally attribute the trend to size. A matched-data scaling ladder (Pythia [18]) is required precisely because the confound (prior sharpening) is itself a function of scale - the very axis being measured. Why naive approaches fail, concretely: aggregate negated accuracy hides per-item prior strength (collapses C1+C2+C3); raw top-1 flip is a step function of the affirmative prior (mostly C2); a single template confounds C1 with readout artifact; and cross-family scaling plots confound size with data/recipe, so the headline inverse-scaling slope is unidentified.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

The unclaimed quadrant is the intersection of three commitments that no prior work has held simultaneously: {a prior-controlled, readout-marginalized, interaction-resolved negation-sensitivity decomposition} x {the cloze inverse-scaling claim of Finding 1} x {a by-construction collapse to the source scalar}. Each neighboring literature touches one or two of these and misses the third, and the near-misses are honest. Probing studies [2][3][6] report AGGREGATE negated accuracy or perplexity with NO per-item affirmative baseline, so "knows the fact well" (C2) and "handles negation well" (C1) are entangled at the point of measurement; they cannot separate prior strength from negation signal because nothing in their pipeline normalizes a negated item against its own affirmative twin. Inverse-scaling work [13][14] documents the trend and even the U-shaped recovery, but treats the metric as GROUND TRUTH - it asks whether the curve goes down and back up, never whether the curve is confounded by prior strength that co-varies with scale. The NLI annotation-artifact literature [23][24] is the closest in SPIRIT: it invented hypothesis-only and artifact baselines precisely to show that a lexical-negation cue spuriously predicts an outcome - but it did so for the CONTRADICTION LABEL in NLI, and never ported the attribution logic to the cloze SCALING claim. The source paper [1] is the most comprehensive synthesis across three categories, yet it reads Finding 1 off a non-prior-controlled scalar; it observes the inverse trend without asking whether sharper priors in larger models manufacture it.

PCNS differs and improves by occupying the empty quadrant deliberately. It introduces a per-item affirmative baseline so C1 is measured as a truth-conditional shift NORMALIZED against what the model already believes, isolating C2 (the prior, which SHARPENS with scale) as a separate, named quantity instead of letting it leak into the sensitivity score. It marginalizes over a readout ENSEMBLE (multiple templates and scoring rules) so fixed-template artifacts cannot masquerade as a capability effect, and it estimates the crossed variance components via REML/LMM over item x readout x model [26] so C1, C2, and the C3 INTERACTION come with uncertainty rather than as point estimates. Crucially, it adds the labeled THIRD component C3 (C1 x C2): does the model fail to negate specifically the facts it knows best? - the single most diagnostic quantity for the inverse-scaling claim, and one absent from every prior study. And it does all of this without breaking continuity with the source: the decomposed profile collapses to the original negated-cloze scalar as recomputed under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled (exact within floating-point tolerance for the by-construction internal recompute; matching Truong et al.'s published numbers only within reproduction tolerance). The reason it has not been solved is that solving it requires holding the collapse discipline AND the decomposition AND the scaling target together - each community had the tools for one corner and the incentive to stop there.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

METHOD.
  (a) MATCHED MINIMAL PAIRS. Build matched affirmative/negated minimal pairs from MKR-NQ [6] and LAMA [11]: each item is an (affirmative, negated) twin differing only by the negation cue, holding subject/relation/object constant so the negation edit is the only manipulated variable.
  (b) PER-ITEM PRIOR ESTIMATE (C2 hook). From the AFFIRMATIVE-ONLY condition, estimate each item's prior as the calibrated log-prob and/or rank of the gold completion (prior CONCENTRATION). This is the model-specific affirmative baseline that scale sharpens.
  (c) PER-ITEM NEGATION EFFECT (C1 hook). Define a direction-correct, prior-normalized shift: the log-odds the model WITHDRAWS from the now-false original answer AND/OR MOVES TOWARD a licensed alternative under "not". Define it so that the RAW scalar and the NORMALIZED effect differ only by the C2 (prior) term - i.e., C1 = raw negation shift minus the prior-attributable component, by construction.
  (d) READOUT ENSEMBLE. Score every item under multiple templates and multiple scoring rules: prob-of-original-completion, top-1 flip, rank shift, and calibrated log-prob [27]; and under cloze vs MCQ vs NLI framings of the SAME item. Readout is a crossed random effect, not a fixed choice.
  (e) CROSSED VARIANCE COMPONENTS. Fit a REML/LMM [26] over item x readout x model to estimate the C1, C2, and C3 effect/variance shares with uncertainty (C3 = the prior x negation interaction term).
  (f) COLLAPSE SWITCH. A single flag sets singleton readout + uniform weights + prior-control disabled; the profile then recomputes the source scalar B1 (collapse target), EXACT within floating-point tolerance.

DATASETS.
  - MKR-NQ [6] PRIMARY cloze sensitivity; LAMA [11] for affirmative baselines / prior estimation.
  - NeQA [14] as an INDEPENDENT inverse-scaling target (Inverse Scaling Prize), to replicate/probe the trend off-distribution from MKR-NQ.
  - Antonym/synonym lexical set from [1] (source category 2).
  - NLI with negation: MoNLI [9], NaN-NLI [7], CondaQA [16], NLI stress test [28]; hypothesis-only / artifact controls in spirit of [23][24].
  - Negated/misprimed probes from Kassner & Schutze [2] as a cross-check.

MODELS.
  - Pythia 70M-12B [18] PRIMARY (matched-data scaling identification - clean capacity axis with data/recipe held fixed).
  - OPT family [21] as a second open ladder.
  - GPT-3 / InstructGPT [19][20], FLAN-T5 / FLAN-PaLM [22], and ChatGPT as reported-result ANCHORS and the instruction-tuning arm (logprobs permitting), to tie back to [1] and test the alleviation note (H3).

METRICS.
  - C1 / C2 / C3 effect and variance shares (with CIs from the LMM [26]).
  - Readout-stability: cross-readout variance of C1 (lower = more genuine).
  - Prior-controlled negation slope vs scale (the re-attributed Finding 1).
  - Collapse: max-abs diff between profile-under-switch and recomputed B1 (~0, float tolerance).
  - Calibration: ECE [27] per model/readout (units-comparability check).

BASELINE LADDER.
  B1 = source scalar recomputed on our pipeline (COLLAPSE TARGET).
  B2 = + per-item affirmative-baseline normalization (prior-control ON).
  B3 = + readout-ensemble marginalization.
  B4 = full PCNS profile with crossed variance components (C1/C2/C3).
  ORACLE = gold per-item prior from held-out probes (UPPER BOUND only, never a deployable system).

FALSIFIABILITY TRIAD.
  (i) BY-CONSTRUCTION COLLAPSE: under the switch, profile == B1 EXACT within floating-point tolerance.
  (ii) DUAL PLACEBO.
       leg-1 STRUCTURE-DESTROYING: shuffle the affirmative<->negated pairing so each negated item is normalized against a MISMATCHED affirmative prior -> the prior-control gain (B2 over B1) must VANISH.
       leg-2 ABSENT-MECHANISM: replace "not" with a MEANING-PRESERVING non-negation edit (insert "indeed"/"really") -> C1 must go to ~0.
  (iii) DOSE-RESPONSE: graded negation DEPTH (single "not" -> double negation -> negation+quantifier -> negation+gradable adjective) yields MONOTONE C1; and prior-strength BINS (binned affirmative concentration) yield MONOTONE C3.

AGGREGATE-vs-DISAGGREGATE DISSOCIATION (at matched compute). On the Pythia ladder [18] at matched compute, the aggregate negated-cloze scalar reproduces inverse scaling while the prior-controlled C1 is FLAT or REVERSED, and the C3 interaction grows with scale -> Finding 1 is REATTRIBUTED to C2/C3 (prior + interaction), and the reattribution is shown NOT to be a function of added parameters/compute.

EXPECTED RESULTS + COMPUTE.
  H1 (artifact): a non-trivial share of Finding 1's inverse-scaling slope is C2/C3; after prior control the negation-attributable inverse-scaling weakens or reverses.
  H2 (interaction): failures concentrate on high-prior items - C3 > 0 and grows with scale (the model fails to negate specifically what it knows best).
  H3 (instruction-tuning): the FLAN/ChatGPT alleviation [1][22] acts primarily by reducing C2/C3 (softening the prior under negation), not by raising raw capacity (C1) - testable directly via the decomposition.
  COMPUTE: INFERENCE-ONLY, no training. We re-score existing benchmarks across a model ladder x readout ensemble: Pythia/OPT run locally; GPT-3/Instruct/FLAN/ChatGPT via API logprob calls. Estimated low TENS of GPU-hours plus API logprob calls - well within academic scale.

## 4. References and Evidence

References (cited entries; numbering inherited from the canonical list):

[1] Truong, Baldwin, Cohn, Verspoor. Language Models Are Not Naysayers: An Analysis of Language Models on Negation Benchmarks. ACL Findings 2023. (SOURCE)

Evidence and provenance notes:

- SOURCE IDENTIFICATION: The target is Truong et al. 2023 [1], an evaluation/analysis paper whose Finding 1 ("larger LMs are more insensitive to negation") is read off a single negated-cloze sensitivity scalar per model. A scaling claim drawn from one metric that conflates >=2 sources of variation is the canonical trigger for the eval-confound decomposition PCNS performs.

- CONFOUND LOGIC IS REAL (PRECEDENT): The NLI annotation-artifact literature [23][24] already proved that a negation word can be a SPURIOUS cue - Gururangan et al. [23] showed negation predicts the contradiction LABEL, and Poliak et al. [24] formalized hypothesis-only baselines to measure it. This establishes the confound mechanism as documented fact; PCNS ports the same attribution logic, for the first time, from the NLI label to the cloze SCALING claim.

- A FACT THE DECOMPOSITION MUST EXPLAIN: Instruction tuning alleviates negation insensitivity [13][22] (U-shaped recovery; FLAN-T5/FLAN-PaLM), and ChatGPT is strong on hard samples [1]. PCNS must explain this mechanistically - its prediction is that the alleviation reduces C2/C3 (softening the memorized prior under negation) rather than raising raw capacity, which the C1/C2/C3 decomposition can test directly.

- PRIOR-PROBING BASELINE GAP: The cloze-probing lineage LAMA [11] -> negated/misprimed probes [2] -> MKR-NQ [6] reports aggregate negated accuracy/perplexity with NO per-item affirmative baseline, so memorization strength (C2) and negation competence (C1) are entangled in every reported number - the precise gap PCNS's matched minimal-pair design closes.

- SCOPE: All cited findings, and PCNS as framed here, are English-only, inherited from the source [1]. Multilinguality is flagged as a limitation / future-work item, NOT as part of the contribution.

- REPRODUCTION DISCIPLINE: The collapse claim is asserted in two tiers - EXACT within floating-point tolerance for the by-construction internal recompute under a singleton readout, uniform weights, and prior-control disabled; and only "within reproduction tolerance" when matching Truong et al.'s PUBLISHED numbers via re-implementation. No claim of exact reproduction of a published scalar is made anywhere.
